data.title: Wave Digital Assets wants DOJ OIG to investigate US Marshals for poor management of seized cryptocurrency.
data.description: Wave Digital Assets has officially asked the Department of Justice Office of the Inspector General to conduct a thorough investigation into the United States Marshals Service's handling of seized cryptocurrency assets. The request comes after numerous issues over the years, including procurement failures and audit findings remaining unresolved. The concern is about custody, oversight, and transparency regarding the approximately undisclosed number of cryptocurrency assets managed by the USMS.
Thailand’s central bank head predicts inflation will hit target in the coming year.

data.title: Sisel International unveils SiselRIPT™ Sunburst with a tropical flavor to enhance performance nutrition portfolio.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, in Springville, Utah, Sisel International introduced SiselRIPT™ Sunburst, a fresh tropical flavor to its line of performance nutrition products. This new addition aims to enhance the company's range of science-backed supplements, reinforcing their dedication to clean and effective supplementation.

data.title: Altimmune priced $75 million registered direct offering of 17,045,454 shares for serious liver diseases drug pemvidutide.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, Altimmune, Inc. (Nasdaq: ALT), a biopharmaceutical company in Gaithersburg, MD, disclosed a new securities purchase agreement with a fundamental institutional investor. The agreement entails the purchase of 17,045,454 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants) through a registered direct offering. This move is projected to generate about $75 million in gross proceeds, excluding fees and expenses. Altimmune is the sole entity offering these shares and pre-funded warrants. The offering is scheduled to be finalized around January 29, 2026, pending customary closing conditions.
Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has asked bankers on Tuesday to ready financing for a slate of energy and infrastructure projects the government will unveil next week-BBG

data.title: Crypto must be ‘essential’ if legal efforts fall short: BitwiseAccording to Bitwise's Matt Hougan, crypto will face challenges without US market structure bill.
data.description: Matt Hougan of Bitwise has highlighted that the cryptocurrency industry may face challenges in dealing with extended regulatory hurdles and doubt if the United States doesn't enact a market structure legislation. According to Hougan, the industry's future could be in jeopardy as it navigates through an uncertain regulatory environment. This warning comes as the sector continues to attract significant attention, with the total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies reaching approximately $2.5 trillion. Hougan's concerns indicate the importance of regulatory clarity to ensure the long-term sustainability and growth of the crypto market.

data.title: Pony.ai partners with ATBB to expand robotaxi services, advancing autonomous driving and urban mobility.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, Pony.ai, a prominent player in the autonomous driving technology sector, revealed a key collaboration with Beijing ATBB Travel & Express Service Co., Ltd. This partnership aims to enhance their premium mobility services. Pony.ai is known for its success in the widespread mass production and commercialization of autonomous driving technology.

data.title: Singapore Eye Research Institute ranks #1 in ophthalmology among non-academic institutes and #2 globally. Landmark achievement in Dec 2025.
data.description: In January 27, 2026, Singapore Eye Research Institute (SERI) made history by ranking as the top non-academic institute in ophthalmology globally and coming in second place overall among all institutes in the same field. This achievement was solidified in December 2025, showcasing the institute's prominence in the eye research sector.

data.title: Woodside Energy's Q4 report shows 198.8 MMboe production, $7.8/boe production cost, $5.05 billion in depreciation, $200 million in exploration expenditure, and ~$850 million in restoration payments.
data.description: Perth, Australia-based Woodside Energy Group (ASX: WDS) (NYSE: WDS) has released its 2025 full-year guidance. The company anticipates a production volume between 192 to 197 million barrels of oil equivalent (MMboe), with a preliminary result of 198.8 MMboe. Unit production cost is expected to range between $7.6 to $8.1 per barrel of oil equivalent, with an approximate value of $7.8. The forecast indicates property, plant, and equipment depreciation and amortization to fall within $4,800 to $5,100 million, with a projected value of around $5,050 million. Exploration expenditure is estimated at $200 million, while payments for restoration are expected to be in the range of $700 to $1,000 million, approximately $850 million. The company's gas hub exposure is set at a percentage of production.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Dogecoin rise with Bitcoin at $89,201.06, Ethereum at $3,007.96, Dogecoin at $0.1250. XRP steady at $1.89.
data.description: Cryptocurrencies, stocks, and precious metals rallied in anticipation of the Federal Reserve's policy decision. Bitcoin rose by 0.98% to $89,201.06, Ethereum increased by 2.84% to $3,007.96, XRP decreased by 0.07% to $1.89, Solana went up by 2.25% to $126.89, and Dogecoin surged by 2.30% to $0.1250. Institutional buying led to the boost in Ethereum. Nearly $300 million was liquidated in the last 24 hours, with $230 million from leveraged short positions. Bitcoin's derivatives market saw a rise in open interest by 3.12%. Despite the gains, the Crypto Fear & Greed Index indicated prevailing fear sentiment. Top gainers in the cryptocurrency market over 24 hours were observed alongside the broader market rally. Share prices of cryptocurrency-related companies like Strategy Inc. and Bitmine Immersion Technologies Inc. also saw increases of 0.62% and 5.50%, respectively.

Taiwan’s benchmark index climbed as much as 1.2%, hitting an all-time high of 32,706.72.
Thai currency rises to 30.845 per dollar, marking a peak not seen since March 2021.
